evals.prompt: Update the Obsidian properties in `+/skill-eval/frontmatter-scalar.md` so the note has `status: active` and `created: 2026-05-01`. Keep the note body unchanged, use the Obsidian CLI, and verify both the final note content and the property values with `obsidian property:read name=<property> file="..."`.
evals.expected_output: The note keeps valid YAML frontmatter, updates the two scalar properties, preserves the body, and the dedicated property-read command returns the expected values.
expectations: The final note frontmatter contains `status: active`.
The final note body still contains the exact line `Keep this body exactly as it is.`

evals.prompt: Fix `+/skill-eval/frontmatter-list.md` so its Obsidian properties use proper YAML list values instead of scalar strings. Set `tags` to `[project/atlas, draft]`, `in` to `[[Maps]]`, and `up` to `[[Sources]]`. Keep the body unchanged and verify both the final note content and the property values with `obsidian property:read name=<property> file="..."`.
evals.expected_output: The note ends with valid YAML frontmatter where list-valued properties are YAML arrays, not comma-separated or wikilink strings, and property-read returns the expected items.
expectations: The final note frontmatter renders `tags` as a YAML list with `project/atlas` and `draft` as separate items.
The final note frontmatter renders both `in` and `up` as YAML lists, each containing exactly one wikilink item.
The final note body still contains the exact line `Keep this body exactly as it is.`
`obsidian property:read` returns two lines for `tags`, one line `[[Maps]]` for `in`, and one line `[[Sources]]` for `up`.

evals.prompt: Update `+/skill-eval/frontmatter-mixed.md` so its Obsidian properties have `aliases` set to `[Idea Verse, Ideaverse Lite]`, `summary` set to `Skill eval fixture`, and `related` set to `[[Atlas]]` and `[[Home]]`. Preserve unrelated content, keep valid frontmatter, and verify both the final note content and the property values with `obsidian property:read name=<property> file="..."`.
evals.expected_output: The note keeps valid YAML frontmatter, preserves existing body content, writes scalar and list properties in the right shape, and property-read returns the expected values.
expectations: The final note frontmatter renders `aliases` as a YAML list with `Idea Verse` and `Ideaverse Lite` as separate items.
The final note frontmatter contains the scalar line `summary: Skill eval fixture`.
The final note frontmatter renders `related` as a YAML list with `[[Atlas]]` and `[[Home]]` as separate items.
The final note body still contains the exact line `Keep this body exactly as it is.`
`obsidian property:read` returns two lines for `aliases`, one line `Skill eval fixture` for `summary`, and two lines for `related`.
